files.purpose: Main API server with auth, drops, products, orders, admin endpoints
Zod schema validation for drops, products, orders, campaigns

issues.title: Massive Monolithic server/index.js
issues.description: server/index.js contains ~4400 lines with mixed concerns: auth, drops, products, orders, admin, pinata, media proxy, contracts
issues.impact: Hard to maintain, test, and debug. Difficult to locate bugs. Poor separation of concerns.
examples: Line 1859-1962: Drop CRUD operations
Line 2336-2974: Product CRUD operations
Line 3012-3435: Order operations
Line 3958-4014: Pinata file upload

issues.title: Duplicate Configuration
issues.description: server/config.js duplicates environment variable loading and constants already in server/index.js. The loadEnvFile function is defined twice.
issues.impact: Maintenance nightmare. Changes to one file won't propagate to another. Source of truth unclear.
issues.recommendation: Delete server/config.js and consolidate all config into a dedicated server/lib/config.js module

issues.title: Extremely Loose RLS Policies
issues.description: RLS policies use WITH CHECK (true) for public write access, allowing any authenticated user to insert/update ANY data

policies.artists: CREATE POLICY 'artists_read_all' ON artists FOR SELECT USING (true) - ANYONE CAN READ
policies.artists_write: CREATE POLICY 'artists_write_all' ON artists FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true) - ANYONE CAN CREATE
policies.artists_update: CREATE POLICY 'artists_update_all' ON artists FOR UPDATE USING (true) - ANYONE CAN MODIFY ANY ARTIST
policies.drops: CREATE POLICY 'drops_write_authenticated' ON drops FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true) - NO ARTIST VALIDATION
policies.drops_update: CREATE POLICY 'drops_update_authenticated' FOR UPDATE USING (true) - ANYONE CAN UPDATE ANY DROP
policies.products: CREATE POLICY 'products_write_authenticated' WITH CHECK (true) - NO CREATOR VALIDATION
policies.orders: CREATE POLICY 'orders_read_all' FOR SELECT USING (true) - ANYONE CAN READ ALL ORDERS
policies.orders_write: CREATE POLICY 'orders_write_all' FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (true) - ANYONE CAN CREATE ORDERS FOR ANYONE

issues.impact: SEVERE SECURITY VULNERABILITY: Users can modify other users' data, read sensitive order information, create fraudulent orders
issues.recommendation:
Implement proper row-level security:
- Artists can only read/modify their own records
- Drops can only be created/modified by artist_id matching current user
- Products can only be modified by creator_wallet matching current user
- Orders should only be readable by buyer and merchant

issues.title: Missing Input Validation in API Endpoints
issues.description: Many endpoints do not validate headers, body structure, or expected types before processing
examples: Line 1762: req.body.wallet not validated before query
Line 1859: drop.title not validated despite schema existing
Line 2336: No userId/creatorWallet validation on product creation
issues.impact: Injection attacks, malformed data in database, crashes from undefined access
issues.recommendation: Apply validateInput(schema, req.body) to all POST/PATCH endpoints. Add middleware for validation.

issues.title: Private Keys in Environment Variables Not Validated at Runtime
issues.description: DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY is loaded but not validated that it's a valid 256-bit hex string until used
issues.impact: If DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY is misconfigured (wrong format, too short), errors only appear when transactions are attempted, not at startup
issues.recommendation: Validate all private keys at server startup with isValidPrivateKey(). Throw immediately if invalid.

issues.title: Error Messages May Leak Sensitive Information
issues.description: Error responses include raw database error messages which may expose schema details
lines: All catch blocks returning error.message directly
issues.example: Line 385: throw new Error(`Unable to verify artist contract metadata columns: ${error.message}`)
issues.impact: Information disclosure: attackers can enumerate column names, table structure, auth mechanisms
issues.recommendation: Log raw errors server-side, return generic client messages: 'Unable to process request. Contact support.'

issues.title: Auth Endpoints Have Duplicate Routes
issues.description: Both /auth/* and /api/auth/* routes serve identical handlers
examples: Lines 1694-1695: POST /auth/challenge and POST /api/auth/challenge (identical)
Lines 1750-1751: POST /auth/verify and POST /api/auth/verify (identical)
issues.impact: Confusing API surface, maintenance burden, potential versioning mismatches
issues.recommendation: Pick one route pattern. Remove duplicates. Standardize on /api/* pattern.

issues.title: Rate Limiting Config Not Environment-Driven
issues.description: Rate limits are hardcoded in code, not configurable via environment
issues.impact: Cannot adjust rate limits in production without code changes and redeploy
issues.recommendation: Move rate limit settings to environment variables (AUTH_CHALLENGE_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS, AUTH_CHALLENGE_RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS)

issues.title: Missing Request Size Limits on JSON Endpoints
issues.description: express.json() is applied globally without size limit specification
issues.impact: Potential DoS attacks with massive JSON payloads
issues.recommendation: Add size limit: app.use(express.json({ limit: '10mb' }))

steps: Create server/lib/media.js - Media proxy and Pinata operations
Update server/index.js to import and register routers
Replace 'WITH CHECK (true)' with actual user/wallet validation
Artists table: auth.uid() = artist.id or wallet matching current_user
Drops table: artist_id matching current user's artist record
Orders table: buyer_wallet = current_user.wallet OR seller verification
Test all edge cases: cross-user access, privilege escalation, data leakage
Use existing Zod schemas from server/validation.js
Return 400 Bad Request with field-level error details

files.status: ISSUES: Type safety, error handling, API request duplication

issues.title: Disabled TypeScript Strict Mode
issues.description: tsconfig.json explicitly disables critical type safety checks
issues.impact: ~200+ instances of 'any' type throughout codebase. No null-safety. Silent bugs from undefined access.
examples: src/lib/db.ts:1 - eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any (entire file)
src/lib/supabaseStore.ts:1 - same disable comment
Multiple 'error: any' catches without type narrowing
issues.recommendation: Enable strict mode gradually: strictNullChecks=true, noImplicitAny=true

issues.title: Massive Type-Unsafe db.ts File
issues.description: src/lib/db.ts is 1500+ lines with 50+ functions, all with 'any' type parameters
functions: Line 445: secureApiRequest<T>() - properly generic but calling code uses 'any'
Line 527: catch(error: any) - no type narrowing
Line 1348: addToWhitelist(entry: any) - no validation of entry structure
Line 1356: updateWhitelistEntry(id: string, updates: any) - untyped updates
issues.impact: Silent errors passing wrong data types to API. Impossible to catch bugs at compile time.
issues.recommendation: Replace all 'any' with proper types from db.ts types section. Create types for API payloads.

issues.title: Unvalidated User Input in Payment Flows
issues.description: CheckoutPage and payment-related code don't validate user input before sending to backend
lines: src/pages/CheckoutPage.tsx: email, phone, address not validated
src/lib/checkout.ts: detectCheckoutCountry() based on IP - no fallback if detection fails
examples: Line 29-36: email, phone, address, postalCode set with setState() without validation
Line 99-105: Sent to backend without type checking
issues.impact: Invalid shipping data, corrupted orders, fraud potential
issues.recommendation: Add Zod validation: validate email regex, phone format per country, address max length

issues.title: Missing Error Boundaries in Complex Components
issues.description: Large pages don't have ErrorBoundary wrappers. One component crash crashes entire page.
issues.impact: White screen of death on any error. No graceful degradation.
issues.recommendation: Wrap pages with ErrorBoundary. Add try-catch in useEffect hooks with setError state.

issues.title: Inconsistent Error Handling Patterns
issues.description: Error handling varies wildly across frontend code
examples: Some components: throw errors (crash)
Others: setError() state (graceful)
Some: console.error() only (silent fail)
Analytics errors silently caught, user never knows
issues.impact: Unpredictable app behavior. Hard to debug user issues.
issues.recommendation: Standardize: implement ErrorManager service. All errors go through: log + setError state + telemetry

issues.title: API Request Duplication in db.ts
issues.description: Multiple similar API calls with nearly identical error handling
examples: getArtistProfile() at line 548 - uses secureApiRequest
saveArtistProfile() at line 573 - same pattern
getArtistDrops() at line 601 - repeats same pattern
Pattern repeats 40+ times with minimal variation
issues.impact: Code bloat (~1500 lines could be ~500). Hard to change shared logic.
issues.recommendation: Create generic API wrapper: apiGet<T>(), apiPost<T>(), that handle auth, errors, types

issues.title: React Hook Dependencies Not Validated
issues.description: Multiple useEffect hooks potentially missing dependencies
examples: DropDetailPage.tsx lines 193-234: useEffect depends on 'drop' but uses dropId - may call API multiple times
ArtistStudioPage.tsx: useCallback without proper dependencies may capture stale closures
ProfilePage.tsx: multiple useEffect hooks updating same state - potential race conditions
issues.impact: Unnecessary re-renders, memory leaks, stale data displayed to users
issues.recommendation: Run eslint react-hooks plugin. Review all useEffect/useCallback dependencies. Add exhaustive-deps rule.

issues.title: Hardcoded Magic Numbers Throughout
issues.description: Constants like timeouts, limits, thresholds scattered in code
examples: secureAuth.ts line 11: AUTH_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000
secureAuth.ts line 12: SIGNATURE_TIMEOUT_MS = 45000
validation.ts line 8: validateFileUpload() allows max 10*1024*1024 bytes
checkout.ts: hardcoded country detection logic
issues.impact: Hard to adjust for different environments. Maintenance nightmare.
issues.recommendation: Create src/lib/constants.ts with all magic numbers. Import and reuse.

steps: Fix all 'any' types: replace with actual types
Add type narrowing with 'is' checks for error handling
recommendations.benefitSize: Massive bug prevention at compile time

steps: Use proper types for each domain
Define error types: NotFoundError, ValidationError, NetworkError, etc.
Update all API calls to throw typed errors
recommendations.benefitSize: Better UX, actionable error messages, debugging

issues.title: Overly Permissive RLS Policies - MAJOR SECURITY FLAW
issues.description: All core tables (artists, drops, products, orders, whitelist) have RLS policies that allow any authenticated user full access

artists.problem: Anyone can read/write/modify ANY artist's data

drops.issue: Artist_id not checked against current user
drops.problem: Can modify other artists' drops. No ownership validation.

products.problem: Can modify other creators' products. No origin validation.

orders.problem: Authenticated user can read ALL orders containing payment info, buyer addresses, etc.

whitelist.problem: Anyone can read entire whitelist, see who's approved

issues.impact: DATA BREACH: Users can access other users' sensitive data. FRAUD: Can manipulate orders. ACCOUNT TAKEOVER: Can modify other artist profiles.

issues.title: Missing Unique Constraints on Key Fields
issues.description: No UNIQUE constraints on business-critical fields

missing_constraints.artists.wallet: Multiple artist records can have same wallet (duplicates)
missing_constraints.products.contract_listing_id: Product IDs not guaranteed unique per contract
missing_constraints.orders.tx_hash: Same transaction could create multiple orders (double-spend)

issues.impact: Data integrity violations, duplicate records, potential duplicate charges

issues.title: Missing Foreign Key Constraints
issues.description: artist_id, product_id, creative_release_id referenced without FK constraints
examples: drops table references artists(id) without FK
products references creative_releases(id) without FK
orders references products(id) without FK
issues.impact: Orphaned records. Cascading deletes not enforced. Data consistency issues.
issues.recommendation: Add ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY

issues.title: Missing Indexes on Filter Columns
issues.description: Some frequently-filtered columns lack indexes

missing_indexes.artists.role: No index for searching by role

issues.impact: Slow queries at scale. O(n) table scans instead of O(log n) index lookups.
issues.recommendation: Add indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status) WHERE status IN ('pending', 'completed')

issues.title: Composite Indexes Not Optimized
issues.description: Some composite indexes may not match query patterns
examples: Line 932: idx_subscriptions_artist_subscriber - but queries may filter by subscriber first
Line 1037: idx_drops_artist_id_status - but most queries filter by status alone
issues.impact: Index not used, fallback to full table scan
issues.recommendation: Analyze query patterns. Reorder index columns to match WHERE clause order.

issues.title: No Soft Delete Strategy
issues.description: When drops/products/artists are 'deleted', they're actually deleted from database
tables_affected: drops (has 'status' field but actual DELETE not prevented)
issues.impact: Loss of historical data. Can't track deleted drops for auditing. External references break.
issues.recommendation: Add 'deleted_at' timestamp column. Convert DELETE to UPDATE deleted_at = NOW(). Filter queries with WHERE deleted_at IS NULL

issues.title: Index Redundancy
issues.description: Some indexes appear to be created multiple times with different names
examples: idx_subscriptions_artist_subscriber created both at line 932 and 937 (slightly different definition)
idx_artists_shares_contract created both at line 288 and 389
issues.impact: Extra storage, slower INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, confusing schema
issues.recommendation: Remove duplicate indexes. Keep only one per unique set of columns.

recommendations.action: Redesign RLS policies with proper security model

policy_changes.drops: artist_id = (SELECT id FROM artists WHERE wallet = current_user_wallet)

steps: Update SUPABASE_SCHEMA.sql with proper RLS logic
Add helper functions for role checking
Verify no data leakage between users

recommendations.action: Add UNIQUE and FOREIGN KEY constraints
steps: ALTER TABLE products ADD FOREIGN KEY (creative_release_id) REFERENCES creative_releases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
ALTER TABLE drops ADD FOREIGN KEY (artist_id) REFERENCES artists(id)
Test with constraint violations to ensure proper error messages

redundancies.description: Environment loading and configuration duplicated across files
All endpoints registered twice with identical handlers
redundancies.impact: Confusing API surface, maintenance burden, potential versioning issues

examples: 40+ functions with nearly identical pattern: select() -> catch() -> return error
All secureApiRequest calls wrapped identically
Error logging/handling duplicated
redundancies.impact: 1500 line file could be 500. Hard to change shared logic.
redundancies.fix: Create generic API client wrapper functions

redundancies.description: Campaign submission handling defined in multiple places
redundancies.impact: Changes to one location not propagated
redundancies.fix: Move all campaign logic to server/campaigns.js, import in server/index.js

redundancies.description: Challenge and verify endpoints defined in both files
redundancies.fix: Choose one location (server/lib/auth.js), register once in index.js

issues.title: Large Client Bundle from Multiple Dependencies
issues.description: Frontend imports many heavyweight libraries without code splitting
libraries_of_concern: react-pdf - Only used in collection view, not code-split
issues.impact: Slow page load on mobile. Large JS to parse/execute.
recommendations: Code-split heavy routes: lazy(() => import('...')) for artist studio, checkout
Use dynamic imports for contract ABIs, only load when needed
Lazy-load ethers: import ethers from 'ethers/lib/index.esm' with dynamic chunks

issues.title: N+1 Query Patterns in supabaseStore.ts
issues.description: Multiple loops with queries inside each iteration
examples: enrichDropsMediaFromMetadata() at line 357 - fetches metadata for each drop individually
enrichArtistsPortfolioEntries() at line 361 - may fetch portfolio items one-by-one
attachArtistsToDrops() at line 435 - loads artists in loop instead of batch
issues.impact: If 100 drops displayed, API makes 100+ requests instead of 1-2 batch requests
recommendations: Use Promise.all() for parallel requests with batchSize limiting
Prefetch/include related data in initial query with select('..., artists(...)')  from Supabase

issues.title: Large Components Without Memoization
issues.description: Complex pages re-render unnecessarily on parent updates
examples: ProfilePage.tsx (600+ lines) - no memo, all children re-render on any state change
ArtistStudioPage.tsx - renders full content even when only one tab visible
DropDetailPage.tsx - fetches linked release data on every render
issues.impact: Laggy UI, unnecessary API calls, slow interactions
recommendations: Extract tab content into separate memoized components
Use useMemo() for expensive derived state

issues.title: No Response Caching Strategy
issues.description: Frontend refetches same data repeatedly (artists, drops, products)
issues.impact: Wasted bandwidth, slower app, more database load
examples: Visiting ProfilePage refetches artist profile even if loaded 1 second ago
Switching between drops fetches all drops again
No Cache-Control headers on GET endpoints
recommendations: Add staleTime/cacheTime to React Query queries
Implement Cache-Control: public, max-age=300 on backend for public data
Cache artist profiles for 5 minutes client-side

issues.title: Synchronous Contract Reads in UI Render
issues.description: Some components may trigger contract reads during render
issues.impact: Blocks UI rendering until blockchain responds
issues.recommendation: Ensure all contract reads are in useEffect, not during render. Show loading state.

optimizations.title: Code-split heavy routes
optimizations.target: Lazy load checkoutPage, artist studio, admin sections

optimizations.title: Batch metadata fetches
optimizations.target: Fetch all metadata in single Promise.all()
optimizations.gain: 90% fewer API calls for heavy pages

optimizations.title: Implement image optimization
optimizations.current: IPFS images loaded directly, no resizing/compression
optimizations.target: Use image CDN with resize transforms. Serve WebP.

issues.title: Row-Level Security (RLS) Not Enforced
issues.description: All RLS policies use WITH CHECK (true), allowing any authenticated user full access to all data
issues.attack_vector:
Authenticated attacker can:
1. Read all user orders with payment info
2. Modify other artists' profiles
3. Steal/modify other users' drops
4. Manipulate orders
5. View all whitelist entries
issues.impact: Complete data breach, account takeover, fraud

issues.title: No CSRF Protection on State-Changing Operations
issues.description: POST endpoints don't validate CSRF tokens or SameSite cookies
issues.attack:
Attacker creates malicious website with: <form action='https://testpop.com/orders' method=POST> ... </form>
When victim visits while logged in, order is silently placed.
issues.impact: Unauthorized state changes on victim's behalf
issues.fix: Add CSRF token validation: express-csrf middleware

issues.title: Private Keys Logged in Error Messages
issues.description: DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY may appear in error logs if transaction fails
issues.risk: If logs are exposed (ELK, CloudWatch, Sentry), private key is compromised
issues.fix: Never log private key. Use redact function: `redact('DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY', secret)`

issues.title: No Input Sanitization on String Fields
issues.description: String inputs (title, description, name) not sanitized before storage
issues.risk: XSS if data rendered without escaping. Injection if used in queries.
issues.example: POST /drops {title: ''} When rendered: alert(1) executes
issues.fix: Use parameterized queries (Supabase does this). Escape output in React (React.createElement escapes by default).

issues.title: JWT Token Expiration Too Long
issues.description: JWT tokens issued but expiration time not found in code review
issues.risk: If token leaked, stolen token valid forever (or very long time)
issues.check: Verify expiresIn is set to reasonable value (15m, 1h max)

issues.title: Weak Nonce/Challenge Generation May Exist
issues.description: Challenge nonce generation stored in database but implementation not reviewed
issues.risk: If nonce is predictable, signature replay attacks possible
issues.check: Verify nonce uses crypto.randomBytes() not Math.random()
issues.fix: import { randomBytes } from 'crypto'; const nonce = randomBytes(32).toString('hex');

issues.title: Missing Rate Limiting on Public Endpoints
issues.description: /health endpoint has no rate limit
endpoints: GET /health (rate limit exists but not configured)
issues.risk: Attackers can scan server availability without limit
issues.impact: Server enumeration, reconnaissance
issues.fix: Apply rate limiter: app.use(limiter) to all public endpoints

issues.title: Sensitive Data in URLs
issues.description: Drop/product IDs in URLs may be sequential or predictable
issues.risk: Attackers can enumerate all drops/products by incrementing ID
issues.impact: Information disclosure of unreleased/draft drops
issues.fix: Use UUIDs instead of integer IDs for public URLs

issues.title: CORS Configuration May Be Too Permissive
issues.description: FRONTEND_ORIGIN environment variable includes multiple origins
issues.risk: If attacker controls one origin, can make requests on behalf of users
issues.check: Verify credentials: false is set if allowing multiple origins
issues.fix: Use explicit origin whitelist. If credentials: true, single origin only.

issues.title: File Upload Validation May Be Bypassable
issues.description: MIME type checked on upload.single('file') but not validated server-side
issues.code: server/index.js line 260-276: allowed MIME types hardcoded
issues.risk: Attacker uploads executable disguised as image
issues.impact: Arbitrary file upload to IPFS
issues.fix: Add server-side magic byte validation using file-type library

issues.title: Sensitive Information Logged
issues.description: Error messages may contain user data, transaction hashes, contract addresses
locations: server/index.js: error.message logged directly in responses
issues.impact: Log injection, information disclosure
issues.fix: Use structured logging with redaction of sensitive fields

steps: 1. Update SUPABASE_SCHEMA.sql with proper RLS policies
2. Create auth context tables to track current user
4. Test with multiple user accounts to verify isolation
3. Create CSRF token on each page load
2. Create redaction function: redact(key, secret, showChars=4)
3. Never log secrets. Only log addresses.
4. Update Sentry config to strip secrets from error reports

issues.title: TypeScript Strict Mode Disabled
issues.impact: Defers all type checking to runtime. Errors caught in production, not development.

issues.title: Untyped Error Catches
issues.risk: Assumes error is Error object. May crash if error is string/null/undefined.
issues.example: src/lib/db.ts line 527: catch(error: any) => error.message could throw 'Cannot read message of null'

issues.title: Function Parameters Typed as 'any'
issues.risk: No compile-time validation of function arguments

issues.title: Generic Type Parameters Not Enforced
issues.example: secureApiRequest<T>() properly generic but calling code uses 'any'
issues.risk: Response type not validated before use

issues.title: Inconsistent Type Definitions
issues: Artist type in db.ts vs artistStore.ts may differ
Drop type has optional fields but code assumes they exist
Product type missing fields that appear in database

issues.title: No Global Error Boundary
issues.impact: Single component error crashes entire app

issues.title: Silent API Failures
locations: supabaseStore.ts line 442: catch(error: any) { ... } - no user notification
artistStore.ts line 329: catch blocks without setError
issues.impact: User sees stale data without knowing API failed

issues.title: Inconsistent Error Messages
patterns: Some: no error message at all
issues.impact: Confusing UX, hard to debug

issues.title: Missing Timeout Handling
issues.issue: Long-running requests (upload, contract calls) may timeout without user feedback
issues.solution: Add AbortController timeout. Show progress. Allow retry.

summary.overall_health: CRITICAL - Production deployment not recommended without fixes
critical_blockers: Row-Level Security (RLS) policies allow any user full access to all data
No CSRF protection on state-changing operations
TypeScript strict mode disabled with 200+ 'any' types
before_production: ✅ Fix RLS policies to enforce data isolation per user
✅ Implement proper error handling and recovery
✅ Add input validation to all API endpoints
✅ Add database UNIQUE and FOREIGN KEY constraints
✅ Implement rate limiting on all endpoints
✅ Add logging redaction for sensitive data
